rationale: The two notes indicate clear conceptual overlap: the citing paper studies how prior/expected difficulty (discrepant fluency) shifts subsequent customization choices, while the cited paper develops a processing-fluency mechanism in sequential ad-to-brand evaluation. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior fluency and sequential-choice research, making the cited work a plausible mechanism/theory antecedent rather than an incidental mention. Given this, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
